© 2015 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
Launching your list – action guide
Taking action…
Let me be clear… building YOUR list is the single most important thing you can do to
create a highly successful business online.
To get you moving in the right direction, we’ve created a number of simple exercises to
help you put the key list building strategies and tactics we cover in this course into
practice. Take your time and work through each task one by one…. you’ll be glad you did!
I recommend printing out this assignment guide and keeping it close at hand as you work
your way through the training videos.
PHASE ONE: Developing Your List Building Strategy
¨ Action Task #1
Identify Your Avatar (Lesson 2)
Brainstorm the type of “Avatar” that personifies the type of person you would like to have
on your email list.
_______________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________
¨ Action Task #2
Getting Connected with Your List (Lesson 3)
Document what you are currently doing to add new names to your list. Assess your
current strategies for keeping your list fresh.
_______________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

© 2015 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
Launching your list – action guide
¨ Action Task #3
Finding Your Niche (Lesson 4)
Complete the “Niche Self Assessment” document to better define the market you are
targeting with your products and services.
¨ Action Task #4
Defining the Avatar of Your Email List (Lesson 5)
Complete the “Define Your Avatar” document. Then, go to Google, research some niche
forums/social networks, and start documenting the hot topics for your market.
¨ Action Task #5
Selecting Your Email List Host (Lesson 6)
We recommend signing up for a 30-Day $1 trial with ProFollow.com (It will redirect you
to Aweber which it’s supposed to do since Profollow is my private version of Aweber).
Also, this is a good time to review the quick start tutorial on setting up an email list with
Profollow.
PHASE TWO: Creating Your List Building System
¨ Action Task #6
Review your Current Assets (Lesson 7)
Review your current assets (email / non-email lists) and make note of those areas you
would like to develop further.

Launching your list – action guide
¨ Action Task #7
Choosing Your Irresistible Lead Magnet (Lesson 8)
Review the “Lead Magnet Ideas” document and brainstorm the type of lead magnet you
think might work well with your audience.
_______________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________
¨ Action Task #8
Setting Up Squeeze Page (Lesson 9)
Using the “Squeeze Page Planner”, map out the core squeeze page elements you want to
include to maximize your lead conversions.
¨ Action Task #9
Testing Your Squeeze Pages (Lesson 10)
Begin to map out the specific elements you might want to split test for your squeeze pages
(i.e. headlines, copy, video, design format, etc).

¨ Action Task #10
Building a Relationship with Your List (Lesson 12)
Review tutorials on how to set up your auto-responder sequence and broadcast emails in
Profollow.
PHASE THREE: Building a Profitable Email List
¨ Action Task #12
Writing Your Autoresponder Sequence (Lesson 14)
Start to map out the first 5-6 autoresponders in your email sequence.

Launching your list – action guide
¨ Action Task #14
Split-testing Emails (Lesson 16)
Take 10 minutes and write 10 possible subject lines you might consider using for future
emails. Remember the DBB (Don’t Be Boring) principle. Also, refer to the “Email Subject
Lines” document attached to Lesson 16 for more inspiration and ideas!
